sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, carb behavior is everything.
sections.subtitleText: 0.3g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for a tuber, carb behavior is everything.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 18g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.
sections.subtitleText: 18g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for tubers, the carb side decides whether the food feels stable or flimsy.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 1.2g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.
sections.subtitleText: 1.2g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is usually limited here, so the other sections have to carry more.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin c at 19% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: potassium at 11% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.
sections.subtitleText: potassium at 11% daily value stands out most. useful, but still pretty limited.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: works across a lot of cooking styles because utility counts for staple carbs. easy comfort-food base with less baggage than junk food because that practical swap value matters. can be quite filling because a useful practical strength when the preparation stays sensible.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side: can go downhill fast when heavily processed because the category has a big preparation trap. satiety payoff depends a lot on format because some versions are much steadier than others. protein support is weak because tubers still need help from the rest of the meal.

closing.summary: Parsnip does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want practical carbs and the rest of the profile is still reasonably clean.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
